context_lines: Hebrews 7:11-12 should be read within the flow of its chapter and the larger argument of Hebrews.
common_interpretation_lines: A change in priesthood. A change in law. The old covenant required a mediating class — men set apart, functioning between God and the people, offering sacrifices the people themselves could not offer. Hebrews' whole argument is that this pointed forward to something, and that something has already arrived:
One priest. Permanent. No successor needed, because he never dies. And the practical consequence for every believer:
